threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 149 goes to central London without a hitch. The locals know it’s just a long, bumpy wait for the Overground!
Last night on the N37, saw a guy trying to serenade the driver with a ukulele. Timing was all wrong, but the other passengers loved it.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a guy arguing passionately with a pigeon on Karl-Marx-Straße, both equally stubborn. Classic Neukölln moment.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you catch a spontaneous open-air rave under the U-Bahn bridge while waiting for the bus. The city's rhythm is like no other.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re from here when they don't flinch at a BART delay but complain about Muni only because they can't trust the 14 to show up.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the Muni at Civic Center today; the driver had to stop for a sizable dog lounging on the tracks. Classic SF chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every street performer knows how to play a Spanish guitar. They don't realize some just mime to the crowd for tips.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot a recent arrival by the way they stand puzzled at the Metro maps, not realizing the trains are on strike until someone points it out.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the green door on Ridley Road is just a door and not the secret to the best jollof rice in the area.
Last night, a guy on the 2 bus serenaded everyone with a full-on opera piece, totally oblivious to the 'WTF' looks. Brixton nights, man.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a kid using an old bicycle with a basket strapped to it, delivering fresh flowers from the market to elderly neighbors. That’s when it hit me.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a late-night Currywurst stand in a former gas station, complete with a disco ball and a hipster DJ spinning vinyl.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they mention the time they dodged a Muni bus while chasing a taco truck down 24th Street.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy serenading a Muni bus with a ukulele while we all waited forever. Only in SF do delays come with a soundtrack.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's all about the plazas, but it’s really those narrow streets lined with hidden bars that capture our true vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their reluctance to try the local bakery; the regulars already know the best croissants are at the tiny shop on Carrer del Nord.
